title: A WomanпїЅs Last Word
content:
I.

В В В В LetпїЅs contend no more, Love,
В В В В Strive nor weep:
В В В В All be as before, Love,
В В В В Only sleep!

II.

В В В В What so wild as words are?
В В В В I and thou
В В В В In debate, as birds are,
В В В В Hawk on bough!

III.

В В В В See the creature stalking
В В В В While we speak!
В В В В Hush and hide the talking,
В В В В Cheek on cheek!

IV.

В В В В What so false as truth is,
В В В В False to thee?
В В В В Where the serpentпїЅs tooth is
В В В В Shun the tree

V.

В В В В Where the apple reddens
В В В В Never pry
В В В В Lest we lose our Edens,
В В В В Eve and I.

VI.

В В В В Be a god and hold me
В В В В With a charm!
В В В В Be a man and fold me
В В В В With thine arm!

VII.

В В В В Teach me, only teach, Love
В В В В As I ought
В В В В I will speak thy speech, Love,
В В В В Think thy thought

VIII.

В В В В Meet, if thou require it,
В В В В Both demands,
В В В В Laying flesh and spirit
В В В В In thy hands.

IX.

В В В В That shall be to-morrow
В В В В Not to-night:
В В В В I must bury sorrow
В В В В Out of sight:

X.

В В В В Must a little weep, Love,
В В В В (Foolish me!)
В В В В And so fall asleep, Love,
В В В В Loved by thee.
meaning:
"A WomanпїЅs Last Word" is a quintessential example of Robert Browning's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
